title: Biarum angustatum
intro: Biarum angustatum, also known as the 'Narrow-leaved Arum Lily' or 'False Arum', is a perennial plant species belonging to the Arum family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Biarum angustatum include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Biarum angustatum is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to harness its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Biarum angustatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the genus Biarum, which belongs to the family Colchicaceae. This family is part of the larger order Liliales. The Colchicaceae family comprises approximately 69 species of plants, with Biarum angustatum being one of them. Biarum angustatum is native to parts of Asia, where it grows in woodland environments. Its classification as a member of the Colchicaceae family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in this group.

side_effects: Biarum angustatum is a plant species known to be potentially toxic due to the presence of calcium oxalate crystals in its tissues.
